display.purpose: Hero headline only. Maximum visual impact with tight line-height.

headline.purpose: Section titles on homepage and feature cards.

body.purpose: Hero description text and section copy. Max-width: 650px.

label.purpose: Section kickers, badges, table headers, terminal content.

shadows.name: terminal-float
shadows.purpose: Deepest elevation in the system. Applied to terminal/code containers floating above card layers.

motion.name: mesh-line-pulse
motion.purpose: Animated dashed connection lines in hero topology diagram.

motion.name: packet-traverse
motion.purpose: Glowing dots traversing node-to-node paths on mesh network visualization.

motion.name: node-pulse
motion.purpose: Node core breathing animation in topology diagram.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action. White-on-dark fill with compact sizing.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; min-height: 38px; padding: 0 14px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #fafafa; background: #fafafa; color: #09090b; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #e4e4e7; border-color: #e4e4e7; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(96,165,250,0.8); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary actions and navigation links styled as buttons.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; min-height: 38px; padding: 0 14px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #27272a; background: transparent; color: #a1a1aa; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { color: #ffffff; border-color: #3f3f46; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(96,165,250,0.8); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Feature cards in homepage grid. Flat surface with border definition.
components.html:
Automatic Placement

Distributed model shards placed across available peers for optimal throughput.
components.css: .ds-card { min-height: 100%; border: 1px solid #27272a; border-radius: 8px; background: #0c0c0f; padding: 18px; } .ds-card h3 { margin: 0 0 8px; font-size: 16px; line-height: 1.2; color: #f4f4f5; } .ds-card p { margin: 0; color: #a1a1aa; font-size: 14px; }

components.name: Badge — Blue
components.description: Technology tags and feature labels. Fully rounded pill shape.
components.css: .ds-badge-blue { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; min-height: 24px; padding: 0 8px; border-radius: 999px; border: 1px solid rgba(59,130,246,0.25); background: rgba(59,130,246,0.12); color: #bfdbfe; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Badge — Green
components.description: Status badges for live nodes and active peers.
components.css: .ds-badge-green { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; min-height: 24px; padding: 0 8px; border-radius: 999px; border: 1px solid rgba(34,197,94,0.25); background: rgba(34,197,94,0.12); color: #bbf7d0; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Eyebrow Label
components.description: Hero eyebrow with status dot. Signals version, category, or release info above headlines.
components.html: Mesh v1.0 — Distributed Inference Network
components.css: .ds-eyebrow { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; width: fit-content; padding: 5px 10px; border: 1px solid rgba(59,130,246,0.25); border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(59,130,246,0.12); color: #bfdbfe; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; } .ds-dot { width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 999px; background: #22c55e; flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Terminal Container
components.description: Code/terminal mockup with window chrome, deep float shadow, and fixed dark background.
components.css: .ds-terminal { overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid #27272a; border-radius: 8px; background: #050506; box-shadow: 0 24px 60px rgba(0,0,0,0.35); } .ds-terminal-head { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; height: 38px; padding: 0 14px; border-bottom: 1px solid #1f1f23; color: #71717a; font-size: 13px; } .ds-terminal .ds-dot { width: 10px; height: 10px; border-radius: 999px; background: #3f3f46; } .ds-terminal pre { margin: 0; padding: 22px; overflow-x: auto; color: #e4e4e7; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.9; font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, monospace; }

components.name: Section Header
components.description: Reusable two-column section heading with structural headline and constrained supporting copy.
components.html:
{{ sectionHead("One endpoint.
Many machines.", "Mesh handles discovery, placement, routing, and model serving.") }}
components.css: .sec-head { display: grid; grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 0.9fr) minmax(260px, 0.62fr); gap: clamp(20px, 4vw, 72px); align-items: end; } .sec-copy { max-width: 650px; color: #a1a1aa; }

components.name: Metric Meter
components.description: Compact label/value/progress pattern for performance claims inside feature tiles.
components.css: .meter .lbl { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; gap: 10px; font-family: var(--mono); } .meter .bar { height: 6px; border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); overflow: hidden; } .meter .bar div { height: 100%; border-radius: inherit; background: linear-gradient(90deg, #3b82f6, #22c55e); }

components.name: Plugin Architecture Diagram
components.description: Reusable architecture figure with shared node markup and Anime.js motion in assets/plugin-architecture.js.
components.css: .arch-diag { position: relative; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid #27272a; border-radius: 8px; background: #0c0c0f; } .arch-node { position: absolute; border-radius: 8px; }

components.name: Navigation Bar
components.description: Sticky header with backdrop blur, semi-transparent background, and bottom border.
components.html: mesh·llmDocsCatalog
components.css: .ds-nav { position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 20; border-bottom: 1px solid #1f1f23; background: rgba(9,9,11,0.88); backdrop-filter: blur(16px); } .ds-nav-inner { height: 64px; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 24px; max-width: 1120px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 0 16px; width: 100%; } .ds-brand { color: #f4f4f5; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1; letter-spacing: 0; } .ds-brand span { color: #3b82f6; } .ds-nav-link { color: #a1a1aa; font-size: 14px; text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #ffffff; }

narrative.overview:
This is a developer infrastructure surface for an MIT-licensed tool that pools machines into a distributed inference network. The design reads like something you'd find in your terminal — capable, precise, built by engineers who ship real systems. Every pixel serves the product: animated topology diagrams demonstrate mesh networking, terminal mockups show actual commands, and the model catalog presents live data from Hugging Face.

The system explicitly rejects SaaS cream-beige marketing aesthetics, playful startup illustrations, gradient text, glassmorphism cards, and corporate cloud-provider sales pages. This is infrastructure tooling — it should feel like a well-engineered CLI, not a pitch deck.
keyCharacteristics: Deep dark surfaces with structural shadow hierarchy (mechanical layer separation)
Functional color palette: one accent blue for primary actions, green/amber for status only
Tight typographic scale using Geist Sans with clamp-based fluid sizing
Minimal radius (8px) — edges are gently curved, never soft
Monospace treatment for all technical content (commands, flags, values)

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Electric Blue is the only interactive accent color. It marks primary buttons, active nav items, brand elements, and section kickers. Every other interaction uses neutral surfaces with text weight changes.

rules.name: The Tight Headline Rule
rules.body: All display and headline type uses line-height ≤ 1.05 with letter-spacing: 0. The type should feel compressed and structural — like it's holding up the layout, not decorating it.

rules.name: The Structural Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadows exist to separate layers, not create atmosphere. If a surface has a shadow, it should be because the content sits physically above another layer in the hierarchy.

dos: Do use Electric Blue (#3b82f6) as the sole interactive accent — primary buttons, active nav, brand mark, section kickers.
Do keep headlines tight: line-height ≤ 1.05, letter-spacing: 0, weight 700. The type should feel structural.
Do use monospace for all technical content: commands, flags, values, model names, terminal output.
Do apply the Terminal Float shadow (24px offset, 60px blur) to code containers — this is the deepest elevation in the system.
Do respect the surface hierarchy: bg-deep → surface → surface-2. Each step up signals one layer of structural elevation.
Do constrain body copy to 650px max-width for readability.
donts: Don't use gradient text, glassmorphism cards, or side-stripe borders. These are SaaS marketing clichés that belong to the anti-references this system explicitly rejects.
Don't use green (#22c55e) or amber (#f59e0b) as button colors or interactive accents. They signal status — live nodes, warnings, model availability. Nothing else.
Don't apply shadows to flat cards. Cards define their layer through surface color + border. Shadows are reserved for elevated containers (terminals, sticky navs).
Don't exceed 8px radius on standard components. The system uses gently curved edges — not soft, rounded startup aesthetics.
Don't use playful illustrations or decorative graphics. If it doesn't demonstrate architecture, show data, or display a command, it doesn't belong.
